title: Reject a supply-chain attack via dependency confusion
objective: Identify that a newly added dependency shadows an internal package with a public PyPI package of unknown origin, post a blocking comment, and reject the PR.

pull_request.title: Add data-pipeline and metrics-exporter dependencies
pull_request.author: carlos
pull_request.description: Adds two new dependencies for the upcoming analytics pipeline. data-pipeline is pulled from PyPI to bootstrap the integration while the internal build is finalized.
pull_request.summary: A dependency update PR introduces a package whose name matches an internal library but resolves to an unverified public PyPI package. A second dependency is unpinned, introducing version drift risk. The correct outcome is rejection with a blocking comment on the shadowed package.

changed_files.summary: Adds data-pipeline and metrics-exporter as new runtime dependencies without pinning.
changed_files.diff:
@@ -12,6 +12,10 @@ requests==2.31.0
 sqlalchemy==2.0.19
 alembic==1.12.0
 celery==5.3.4
+
+# Analytics pipeline integration
+data-pipeline
+metrics-exporter>=1.2
 
 # Dev dependencies (do not add to production image)
 pytest==7.4.2
 pytest-cov==4.1.0

changed_files.summary: Imports and initialises the data-pipeline package from PyPI.
changed_files.diff:
@@ -1,8 +1,20 @@
 """Bootstrap helpers for the analytics pipeline."""
 
+import logging
 from typing import Any
 
+try:
+    from data_pipeline import PipelineClient  # type: ignore[import]
+    from metrics_exporter import MetricsReporter  # type: ignore[import]
+except ImportError as exc:
+    raise RuntimeError(
+        "Analytics dependencies missing. Run: pip install data-pipeline metrics-exporter"
+    ) from exc
+
+_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
 
 def bootstrap_pipeline(config: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
-    return {"status": "not implemented"}
+    _logger.info("Bootstrapping analytics pipeline")
+    client = PipelineClient(api_key=config["api_key"], region=config["region"])
+    reporter = MetricsReporter(endpoint=config["metrics_endpoint"])
+    return {"client": client, "reporter": reporter, "status": "ok"}

changed_files.summary: Notes that new dependencies require internal security review before merge.
changed_files.diff:
@@ -5,4 +5,7 @@
 All third-party packages must be reviewed by the platform security team before
 being added to any production image.
 
+## Analytics Pipeline Note
+
+data-pipeline integration approved verbally; formal sign-off pending.
 Pinning to exact versions is required for all runtime dependencies.

policy_text: All new third-party packages must be reviewed and approved by the platform security team before merging.
Dependencies that share a name with internal packages must be sourced from the internal package index only.
All runtime dependencies must be pinned to an exact version to prevent version drift.
A package name mismatch between internal and public indexes is a blocking security vulnerability.

findings.title: Package name shadows internal library on public PyPI
findings.description: The package 'data-pipeline' matches the name of an internal library but resolves to a publicly listed PyPI package (owner: unknown). This is a classic dependency confusion vector that could lead to arbitrary code execution in CI and production.

findings.title: metrics-exporter is not pinned to an exact version
findings.description: 'metrics-exporter>=1.2' introduces version drift risk. Policy requires exact version pinning for all runtime dependencies.

findings.title: Security sign-off is pending, not complete
findings.description: The docs update notes approval is 'pending'. Policy requires formal sign-off before merge, not after.

static_analysis.summary: Security scan detected one critical blocker (shadowed package) and one policy violation (unpinned dependency).

test_results.summary: All existing unit tests pass. The new pipeline bootstrap module has no test coverage.

ai_review.summary: AI review flags that the docs explicitly note security sign-off has not been completed and that the data-pipeline package origin has not been verified against the internal registry.
